title: Relations, Rows, Domains, Keys, and Missing Information · Cardinality Forensics
summary: Support tickets, teams, members, and events must become a precise relational model without duplicate identity or invented missing values. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a key-and-null-aware relational model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Explain relations, tuples, attributes, domains, degree, cardinality, and why row order is not relational meaning.
Distinguish superkeys, candidate keys, primary keys, alternate keys, and stable surrogate identifiers.
Model optional and required references with foreign keys and predict update or delete consequences.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Relation Domain
steps.instruction:
debug case — Model teams, members, tickets, and events where members may be unassigned, ticket titles repeat, and one ticket has 6 events. Declare row grain, candidate keys, cardinalities, NULL meaning, dependencies, and lossless relationships. Competency probe: Explain relations, tuples, attributes, domains, degree, cardinality, and why row order is not relational meaning.

Inspect the Relation Domain trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Relation Domain.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Table Grain
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-table-grain-1dhijf" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; relation grain, key, reference, or NULL evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Table Grain.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Table Grain into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Table Grain, keep Table Grain connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-table-grain-1dhijf
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT tickets.ticket_id, members.name AS owner_name
FROM tickets
LEFT JOIN members ON members.member_id = tickets.owner_id
ORDER BY tickets.ticket_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Table Grain structural check is only one gate. a key-and-null-aware relational model must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
